items.title: Federal update including update on relevant court cases
items.summary: The City Manager and Law Department briefed the Council on federal policy developments and ongoing court cases that involve or affect Cambridge, including sanctuary jurisdiction, immigration enforcement conditions on federal grants, and SNAP funding litigation. The CMA was unanimously placed on file.

items.title: Review the City's digital equity work and report back on internet access programs by June 2026
items.summary: Asks the City Manager to review the City's digital equity work to date, evaluate existing internet access programs, propose ways to better meet residents' digital needs, and report back to the Council by June 2026. Charter Right exercised by Councillor Al-Zubi; no final vote at this meeting.
items.body: That the City Manager ask the appropriate departments to review the city's digital equity work to date, including the study's recommendations and all steps taken since the study conclusion in order to update the evaluation of existing internet access programs, assessing whether residents' digital needs are or could be better provided for and to propose how we can better meet their needs and to report back to the Council by June 2026 on internet access programs. COUNCILLOR ZUSY, COUNCILLOR NOLAN, COUNCILLOR SOBRINHO-WHEELER, VICE MAYOR AZEEM. POR 2026-08. RESULT: Charter Right exercised by Councillor Al-Zubi

vote.result: Charter Right exercised by Councillor Al-Zubi

history.action: Charter Right exercised by Councillor Al-Zubi

rationale.doc.status:charter_right_exercised: RESULT: Charter Right exercised by Councillor Al-Zubi
rationale.doc.sponsor:azeem: VICE MAYOR AZEEM listed as sponsor

items.title: Incorporate plant-based purchasing and food practices into Sustainable Cambridge initiative
items.summary: Asks the City Manager to incorporate plant-based solutions into the Sustainable Cambridge initiative, adopt plant-based purchasing for City-operated events, and use municipal channels to promote sustainable, affordable food and drink practices. Adopted as Amended unanimously.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments regarding incorporating plant-based solutions into the Sustainable Cambridge initiative, incorporating plant-based purchasing practices in City-operated events, and using municipal communications channels to promote sustainable and affordable food and drink practices throughout the City. COUNCILLOR NOLAN, COUNCILLOR SOBRINHO-WHEELER, COUNCILLOR MCGOVERN, MAYOR SIDDIQUI. POR 2026-16. RESULT: Adopted as Amended [9-0-0]
amendments: Adopted as Amended (text of amendment not specified in Final Actions)

rationale.topic.area:climate: plant-based purchasing tied to Sustainable Cambridge sustainability initiative
rationale.topic.area:public_health: plant-based food practices framed as public-health/sustainability

items.title: Prioritize Harvard Square pedestrianization pilots in 2026; consider restoring two-way JFK Street
items.summary: Asks the City Manager to prioritize 2026 pedestrianization pilots in Harvard Square without using a consultant for an extensive study, and to consider further pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street by restoring two-way traffic on JFK Street. Adopted unanimously.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments to prioritize pilot projects in 2026 without the use of a consultant for an extensive pedestrianization study, and in the medium-term consider how additional pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street by restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street could enhance the Square. COUNCILLOR NOLAN, COUNCILLOR SIMMONS, MAYOR SIDDIQUI, COUNCILLOR ZUSY. POR 2026-17. RESULT: Adopted [9-0-0]

items.title: Lower City Hall flag to half-staff in remembrance of Renee Good, Alex Pretti, and victims of immigration enforcement
items.summary: Late-filed policy order directing the flag at Cambridge City Hall to be flown at half-staff in remembrance of Renee Good and Alex Pretti, individuals who lost their lives at the hands of federal immigration enforcers, and people enduring federal immigration enforcement, for at least seven days. Adopted as Amended unanimously.
items.body: That the City Manager be and hereby is requested to direct the relevant City staff and departments to order that the flag at Cambridge City Hall, the main administrative municipal building, be flown at half-staff as soon as practicable in somber remembrance of and sympathy with Renee Good and Alex Pretti and their friends and families, the dozens of individuals who have lost their lives at the hands of federal immigration enforcers, and the people enduring the brutality of the Trump Administration's illegal and violent efforts for at least seven days. COUNCILLOR SIBRINHO-WHEELER, COUNCILLOR MCGOVERN, COUNCILLOR AL-ZUBI, MAYOR SIDDIQUI, COUNCILLOR NOLAN. POR 2026-27. RESULT: Adopted as Amended [9-0-0]
amendments: Adopted as Amended (text of amendment not specified in Final Actions)

items.title: Comprehensive review of safety and security at Cambridge City Hall
items.summary: Asks the City Manager to conduct a comprehensive review of safety and security at Cambridge City Hall - physical access controls, visitor screening, on-site security staffing/training, emergency response, and surveillance technologies. Charter-righted by Councillor Simmons on Jan 12; adopted as amended 7-1-1, with Sobrinho-Wheeler in the negative and Al-Zubi present.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to conduct a comprehensive review of existing safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall, which shall include an assessment of physical access controls, visitor screening practices, on-site security staffing and training, emergency response and evacuation protocols, and the use of security technologies such as surveillance systems and alarm monitoring. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL JANUARY 12, 2026. RESULT: Adopted as Amended [7-1-1-0] YEAS: Councillor Simmons, Councillor Mcgovern, Vice Mayor Azeem, Councillor Nolan, Mayor Siddiqui, Councillor Flaherty, Concillor Zusy NAYS: Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler PRESENT: Councillor Al-Azubi

items.title: Cambridge Street Corridor zoning: amend Map and Articles 3, 17, 19 to establish four new districts
items.summary: Ordinance amending the Cambridge Zoning Map and Articles 3.000, 17.000, and 19.000 to establish four new zoning districts for the Cambridge Street corridor. Passed to second reading Dec 8 2025 and tabled Dec 22 2025; ordained as amended 6-3, with Al-Zubi, Flaherty, and Zusy in the negative.
items.body: Ordinance has been received from Interim City Clerk Paula M. Crane, relative to amend the Zoning Map and Articles 3.000, 17.000, and 19.000 of the Cambridge Zoning Ordinance as follows with the intent of establishing four new zoning districts for the Cambridge Street corridor. PASSED TO A SECOND READING AS AMENDED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 8, 2025. ELIGIBLE TO BE ORDAINED DECEMBER 22, 2025. TABLED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025. RESULT: Ordained as Amended [6-3-0] YEAS: Councillor Simmons, Councillor Mcgovern, Vice Mayor Azeem, Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Councillor Nolan, Mayor Siddiqui NAYS: Councillor Al-Zubi, Councillor Flaherty, Concillor Zusy

items.title: Joint Housing + Finance Committee report: residential development incentives feasibility
items.summary: Report from a joint Housing + Finance Committee hearing held December 3, 2025 on the feasibility and impacts of residential development incentives (tax incentives, public equity financing, reduced fees and requirements) on housing production and city finances. Report Accepted and Placed on File unanimously.
items.body: The Housing Committee and Finance Committee held a joint public hearing on December 3, 2025 to discuss the feasibility and potential impacts of implementing residential development incentives - such as tax incentives, public equity financing, and reduced fees and requirements - on housing production and city finances. CC 2026-01. RESULT: Report Accepted and Placed on File [9-0-0]

vote.result: Report Accepted and Placed on File [9-0-0]

history.action: Joint Housing + Finance Committee public hearing

rationale.doc.status:report_accepted: RESULT: Report Accepted and Placed on File [9-0-0]

items.title: Public Safety Committee report: Automatic License Plate Reader (ALPR) deployment review
items.summary: Report from a Public Safety Committee public hearing held December 9, 2025 further discussing the implications of deploying automatic license plate readers (ALPRs) and whether adjustments should be made since approval in February 2025 (CMA 2025 #257, City Council October 20, 2025). Report Accepted and Placed on File unanimously.
items.body: The Public Safety Committee held a public hearing on December 9, 2025 to further discuss the implications of deploying automatic license plate readers (ALPRs) and to discuss whether adjustments should be made related to the deployment of ALPRs since approval in February of this year, CMA 2025 #257, which was in City Council on October 20, 2025. CC 2026-03. RESULT: Report Accepted and Placed on File [9-0-0]

items.title: Joint Health & Environment + Housing Committee report: solar energy zoning language
items.summary: Report from a joint Health & Environment + Housing Committee public hearing held December 11, 2025 reviewing POR 2025 #137, which asks the Community Development Department to draft zoning language to reduce the impact on solar energy systems. Report Accepted and Placed on File unanimously.
items.body: The Health and Environment Committee and Housing Committee held a joint public hearing on December 11, 2025 to review and discuss POR 2025 #137, which asks the Community Development Department to draft zoning language to reduce the impact on solar energy systems, based on the recommendations discussed in the September 16, 2025 Health and Environment Committee meeting. CC 2026-04. RESULT: Report Accepted and Placed on File [9-0-0]

items.title: Make city-owned parking lots, including Tobin/Darby Vassal complex, available after-hours to residents
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to work with the School Department, DPW, and other departments to make city-owned parking lots, with focus on school complexes including the under-construction parking at the Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex, available for after-hours use by residents. Tracked on the awaiting report list.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to ensure that all city owned parking lots, with a focus on school complexes, including the still under construction parking at Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex, could be made available for after-hours use by residents. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-29. Councillor Nolan. AR 2026-02

items.title: Formal policy on City role and financial responsibilities for large nonprofit public events
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to consult with City departments including the City Solicitor's Office to establish a formal policy that clearly defines the City's role and financial responsibilities in supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based nonprofits. Tracked on the awaiting report list.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to consult with the appropriate City departments, including the City Solicitor's Office, to establish a formal policy that clearly defines the City's role and financial responsibilities in supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based non-profit and not-for-profit organizations. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-48. Councillor Simmons, Councillor McGovern. AR 2026-03

rationale.doc.kind:AR: Section XIV. AWAITING REPORT LIST, item 2

items.title: Streamline permitting process: central role, unified online portal, standardized timelines
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to streamline the permitting process - including a central role dedicated to permitting, a unified online permitting portal, and standardized timelines - and report back not later than March 2026. Tracked on the awaiting report list.
items.body: Streamline the permitting process, including but not limited to creating a central role dedicated to the permitting process, the creation of a unified online permitting portal, and standardized timelines, and report back to the City Council not later than March 2026. Original Awaiting Report AR-25-68. Vice Mayor Azeem, Councillor Zusy, Councillor McGovern, Councillor Nolan. AR 2026-05

items.title: Evaluate and install speed humps on Fayerweather Street between Walnut and Field
items.summary: Awaiting Report directing the Department of Transportation to evaluate and install a series of speed humps along Fayerweather Street, particularly between Walnut Street and Field Street. Tracked on the awaiting report list.

items.title: Review legal parameters and oversight for visitor information kiosk and Tourism Marketing District
items.summary: Awaiting Report directing the City Solicitor and Community Development Department to report back on (i) legal parameters around city funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District restrictions, and (ii) oversight and accountability for the visitor information kiosk. Tracked on the awaiting report list.
items.body: Direct the City Solicitor and the Community Development Department to review and report back to the Council on (i) legal parameters related to City funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District restrictions, and (ii) oversight and accountability related to the operation of the visitor information kiosk, and related governance expectations, including alignment with City priorities. Original Awaiting Report ID #31841. Councillor Simmons, Councillor Flaherty. AR 2026-09
